mountain, the mines of beryl, conch shells and pearls, AtrPs her-
mitage, the mariners, Dharmapattana (Dharmapuri ?), the islands,
Ganarajya, Krsna, Vellum, the Pisikas, the ^urpa mountain, mount
Kusuma, Tumbavana (a forest), the Karmaneyakas, the Southern
Ocean, Tapasasrama (a place abounding in hermitages), the Rsikas,
Kaftci, Marucipaitana, Ceryaryakas (or Ceryas i.e. people of Cera
or Kerala, kingdom, and the Aryakas), the Sirhhalas, Rsabhas,
Baladevapattana (the modern Valarpatnam in Malabar ?),Dandaka
forest, the whale-eaters, the Bhadras, Cutch, elephant caves (where
they are caught) and the TamraparnI river.
[We have to find out the reason for the separate mention of
Lanka and Simhala, Fleet suggests ^LaAka seems to denote here not
the island of Ceylon, but its capital city, because it provides the
Hindu prime meridian.]
